ops.title: ship: survive non-fast-forward push rejections (retry fetch+rebase; git-fallback parity)
ops.description: `ccx vcs ship` hit a raw non-fast-forward push rejection twice on 2026-07-23 (commits a6eeded and d494104, both in /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-context, plain-git clone) when concurrent sessions advanced origin/main mid-ship. AGENTS.md documents ship as fetching first and rebasing onto the target bookmark in-call, but either the fetch→push window races with no retry, or that path only exists in jj mode and the git fallback lacks it. Manual recovery each time: cherry-pick the formed commit onto origin/main in a temp worktree, push from there, `git reset --keep origin/main` in the checkout (leaves concurrent lanes' dirty files untouched). Fix directions: retry the fetch+rebase+push cycle at least once on a non-fast-forward rejection, and give the git fallback the same remote-update handling as the jj lane. See the two 2026-07-23 papercut entries for full context.

ops.text: A ship whose push is rejected non-fast-forward (remote advanced mid-call, no conflict) recovers in-call: refetches, rebases the formed commit onto the updated target, pushes, and watches CI — no manual worktree cherry-pick
The recovery path works in a plain-git (non-jj) clone
A rebase that would conflict still rolls back and reports, leaving the working copy untouched
